document_conditional_note: Visa-exempt for the Schengen 90/180 rule ONLY when traveling on a biometric passport (Annex II, Regulation (EU) 2018/1806). A non-biometric passport from this nationality remains visa-required as shown.

Visa-exempt for the Schengen 90/180 rule ONLY when traveling on a biometric passport (Annex II, Regulation (EU) 2018/1806; Serbia carries a further named exclusion for passports issued by the Serbian Coordination Directorate). A non-biometric passport from this nationality remains visa-required as shown.

Residence beyond 3 months is conditional (Directive 2004/38/EC Art. 7): worker or self-employed status, OR sufficient resources plus comprehensive sickness insurance, OR enrolled-student status with resources and insurance, OR a qualifying family member of someone who is. The `exempt` tier above is unconditionally true regardless of whether Art. 7 is ever met -- Art. 6 grants entry and residence up to 3 months "without any conditions or any formalities other than the requirement to hold a valid identity card or passport." This note discloses an additional, better-secured right some readers can reach on top of the baseline tier, never a condition on the baseline itself (8Z.2 Sense A, not Sense B -- see this row's own notes). A host state may require registration after arrival (Art. 8), but the Directive states plainly that holding a registration certificate "may under no circumstances be made a precondition for the exercise of a right" -- a formality, not a gate.

Since 2026-02-05, air-entry travelers of this nationality may use Mexico's SAE electronic pre-authorization (Sistema de Autorizacion Electronica) instead of a full consular visa -- single-entry, valid 30 days from issuance for use, stay length set case-by-case by the immigration officer at arrival. Air entry ONLY -- land/sea entry still requires a full visa for this nationality.

China/India nationals are NOT eligible for Argentina's AVE electronic authorization; they enter visa-exempt for 90 days if holding a valid US visa (B1/B2, B2, J, B1, O, P, E, or H-1B) or US green card. This base row is the true default absent that facility.
